constraints: No persisted graph node or edge writes.
No retrieval, embedding, reranking, or production index mutation.
No automatic inclusion in context packs.
All recommendations must be provenance-backed by existing relations, edge records, lineage data, or explicitly marked non-authoritative edge seeds.
next_action: Implement the admitted read-only graph neighborhood advisor diagnostic slice. Maintain the diagnostic-only boundary: no runtime context selection changes, no provider prompt changes, no graph writes, no retrieval or embedding calls, and no automatic inclusion in context packs.
objective: Define the first governed slice for GAP-0002 by adding a read-only graph neighborhood diagnostic/advisor that can report candidate neighboring artifacts, relation paths, scope interpretation, token estimates, and inclusion/exclusion rationale without changing runtime context selection.
proposed_change: Add a deterministic, read-only diagnostic surface for graph-local scoped memory. The slice should inspect existing artifact lineage/front-matter relations, committed EDGE records when present, and non-authoritative future edge seeds, then produce an advisor report for a target task or artifact. The report must be explanation-only and must not write graph artifacts, mutate context manifests, change provider prompts, call retrieval or embedding services, or alter selection.
rationale: GAP-0002 is open and records graph-local scoped memory context selection as a missing capability.
COMP-0025 shows both providers agreed this gap is distinct from CAP-0001 through CAP-0004.
The development direction recommends lightweight graph schemas and simple neighborhood queries before full graph integration.
A read-only diagnostic follows the established safety pattern used for L0 diagnostics before runtime behavior changes.
risk: The main risk is accidentally turning graph-neighborhood advice into runtime selection. The first slice must remain diagnostic-only and must not modify manifests, prompts, retrieval, embeddings, or persisted graph state.
success_criteria: A proposed diagnostic/advisor contract is represented in tests and source code without runtime selection effects.
Given deterministic fixture artifacts and edges, the advisor reports neighboring candidates with relation-path explanations.
The advisor distinguishes authoritative committed relations from non-authoritative future edge seeds.
The advisor reports token estimates and inclusion/exclusion rationale under a stated budget.
Existing latest-context behavior remains unchanged by default.
Self-model and audit tests remain green.
title: Add read-only graph neighborhood advisor diagnostic
